themeWords.meaning: to reign, be king, become king

sentenceTheme: The LORD, enthroned between the cherubim, reigns with holiness and justice, calling all peoples to exalt and worship Him.

verses.text: The LORD reigneth; let the people tremble: he sitteth between the cherubims; let the earth be moved.

highlightedWords.meaning: cherub, celestial being attendant upon God

verses.text: The LORD is great in Zion; and he is high above all the people.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zion, the hill of Jerusalem; figuratively, the city of God

verses.text: Let them praise thy great and terrible name; for it is holy.
The king's strength also loveth judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.
Exalt ye the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.

highlightedWords.meaning: to be high, exalt, lift up

verses.text: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.

highlightedWords.meaning: Moses, the great lawgiver and leader of Israel
priest, one who officiates in religious service

verses.text: He spake unto them in the cloudy pillar: they kept his testimonies, and the ordinance that he gave them.
Thou answeredst them, O LORD our God: thou wast a God that forgavest them, though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions.

highlightedWords.meaning: to lift, bear, take away; to forgive

verses.text: Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the LORD our God is holy.

highlightedWords.meaning: to bow down, prostrate oneself, worship
holiness, a holy thing or place; sacred
